thesis_update_md: Short-term price spike risk confirmed; relief levers exist but are partial and lagged.
summary_md: A Hormuz disruption would lift crude sharply on a risk premium [s01][s04]. Relief depends on OPEC spare capacity [s02], SPR drawdown timing [s03], and limited bypass throughput [s05].

key_findings.finding: Risk premium already visible in tanker freight rates
OPEC+ spare capacity near 4 mb/d offers partial offset
SPR drawdown is material but lagged by logistics
Goreh-Jask bypass cannot replace full Hormuz throughput

scenarios.name: Brief closure
scenarios.summary: Days-long disruption, sharp spike then partial mean reversion.

scenarios.name: Sustained blockade
scenarios.summary: Weeks-long, structural repricing, coordinated SPR + OPEC response.
